must_link: The vendor says the orchard sensor kit can cut water use by twenty percent in dry weeks.
Water savings can happen, but the number depends on soil and maintenance discipline.
The vendor says the orchard sensor kit can cut water use by twenty percent in dry weeks.
The local plan gives policy incentives for sensor adoption, but it does not guarantee water savings.
The dashboard map on screen is useful, but the maintenance cost is not discussed in the briefing.
Several growers say maintenance and calibration are the hidden cost, not the hardware price.
Their case study claims a yield bump after the software started changing irrigation zones.
This might be wrong, but local salinity may explain the yield bump more than the sensor software.
